feed.title: 4chan
feed.subtitle: Newest threads from selected worksafe 4chan boards (news, g, o, tv, v, mu, vip) via the read-only JSON API, plus the official 4chan blog. Board posts are user-generated and unmoderated; each title is prefixed with its board.

entry.title: /trash/ Off-Topic: /flg/ - F-List General #8340 Short King Edition Previous Thread: >>83662550 F-li…
entry.content: /flg/ - F-List General #8340 Short King Edition Previous Thread: >>83662550 F-list is a website for people interested in erotic roleplaying, and creating character profiles to find like-minded partners with similar interests and fetishes to roleplay with. It features a webchat (which can be used for textual roleplaying), group forums and more kinks and depravity than you can memorize. The purpose of this thread is to serve as a place where roleplayers can freely share and advertise their profile

entry.content: Americans are really proud when it comes to the proximity fuze®. Many of their articles call it the tech that 'won WW2'. His will was written so that his authorship of the Report would only be p

entry.title: /o/ Auto: Bigger car = more respect on the road
entry.content: Honestly there's a hierarchy on the roads and you should just get the biggest car that you can afford. You can even dominate exotic cars and rich people. I find that more cars are willing to cut me off while I'm in my corolla than when I'm in my pickup. Driving dynamics mean nothing when people don't respect you on the roads in your beta feminine tiny miata.

entry.title: /g/ Technology: LeetCode Daily Problem
entry.content: Today's is a medium... you should be able to complete it easily, right /g/? You are given an integer array nums and an integer target. Return the number of subarrays of nums in which target is the majority element. The majority element of a subarray is the element that appears strictly more than half of the times in that subarray. https://leetcode.com/problems/count -subarrays-with-majority-element-i/ ?envType=daily-question

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: Star Fox 64
entry.content: >Game is very short but insanely replayable, can be beaten in a single sitting >Has lots of optional hidden routes meaning that if you only play it once you'll miss most of the game Unironically KINO game design. Such a shame modern games can't really do this unless its a randomized run based game because gamers will immediately start screeching about value.

entry.title: /g/ Technology: >be me >trying to dump custom sysex patches from a roland juno >have to download…
entry.content: >be me >trying to dump custom sysex patches from a roland juno >have to download desktop software last updated during the bush administration >drivers keep crashing on win 11 >fuck this, i'm a web dev spent the last month building a web-midi tool that does this natively in chrome/edge. no installs, no legacy driver shit, just dumps sysex direct to a web app. test it out if you have old vintage gear laying around. let me know if it bugs out on your midi interface: knob.monster

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: >DESIGN IS LAW >*Sees Quake 2* >*Wipes glasses* HOMMINA HOMMINA HOMMINA AWOOGA >…
entry.content: >DESIGN IS LAW >*Sees Quake 2* >*Wipes glasses* HOMMINA HOMMINA HOMMINA AWOOGA >Fuck all the work we've done, we need a shiny new graphics engine. I'm sure porting our work in Quake 1's engine to Quake 2's will be easy and our one asian programming guy won't quit Was Romero retarded?
